evals.prompt: I need to build a Node.js app that takes two RTMP streams (from OBS) and arranges them side by side in a grid, then streams the combined output to YouTube Live via RTMP. Write the full working code.
evals.expected_output: A complete Node.js script using @swmansion/smelter-node that registers two RTMP server inputs, uses a Tiles component for the side-by-side layout, and outputs via rtmp_client to YouTube.
expectations: Uses import from @swmansion/smelter-node (default import for Smelter class)
Calls smelter.init() before registering inputs or outputs
Registers inputs with type rtmp_server
Uses the Tiles component from @swmansion/smelter for the side-by-side layout
Registers output with type rtmp_client and provides a url field
Includes video encoder configuration (e.g. ffmpeg_h264) and resolution in the output
Includes audio configuration with aac encoder in the output (AAC is required for RTMP)
Calls smelter.start() to begin processing

evals.prompt: How do I render a lower-third text overlay with a semi-transparent dark background on top of a live video stream in Smelter? I'm using Node.js.
evals.expected_output: Code showing a View with absolute positioning containing the text overlay, placed on top of an InputStream, with proper styling for the semi-transparent background.
expectations: Uses a View component as the root container with explicit width and height
Uses InputStream component to display the video stream
Uses Text component from @swmansion/smelter for the lower-third text
The text overlay is positioned at the bottom of the screen using absolute positioning (top/left/bottom/right props on a child View)
Includes a backgroundColor with alpha channel for semi-transparency (e.g. #00000080 or similar)
Text component has fontSize set (fontSize is required in TextStyleProps)
The code shows the correct import pattern from @swmansion/smelter

evals.prompt: I want to build a browser-based video conferencing app where each participant's camera is shown in a grid. I don't want to run a separate server. Which Smelter package should I use and how do I set it up?
evals.expected_output: Recommends @swmansion/smelter-web-wasm since no server is needed, explains it runs in a Web Worker, mentions Chrome-only limitation, and shows setup with camera inputs.
expectations: Recommends @swmansion/smelter-web-wasm as the correct package (no server needed)
Mentions that WASM runtime is Chrome-only
Shows how to register camera inputs using type camera
Uses Tiles component for the participant grid layout
Uses canvas or stream output type (these are the WASM output types)
Mentions or shows that WASM runs in a Web Worker

evals.prompt: I have an MP4 video file and I want to use Smelter to add a title card at the beginning (first 3 seconds show title text, then the video plays). Output to a new MP4 file. Node.js please.
evals.expected_output: Uses OfflineSmelter with the render() method, registers MP4 input, uses Show or SlideShow for time-based sequencing, and outputs to MP4.
expectations: Uses OfflineSmelter from @swmansion/smelter-node (not the live Smelter class)
Calls smelter.render() instead of registerOutput + start (OfflineSmelter API)
Registers input with type mp4
Uses a time-based mechanism for the title card (Show component with useAfterTimestamp, or SlideShow with Slide components)
Uses Text component for the title text
Output type is mp4 with serverPath specified
Includes video encoder and resolution configuration
